description: Content in this archive draws on the following categories of primary and secondary sources. Where specific documents are reproduced, they are cited within their respective entries.

sources.title: Der Zweite Weltkrieg (The Second World War)
sources.author: Basil Liddell Hart
sources.notes: Comprehensive operational history; includes interviews with surviving German generals.

sources.title: Inside the Third Reich
sources.author: Albert Speer
sources.notes: First-hand account of the Nazi leadership by Hitler's armaments minister.

sources.title: Panzer Leader
sources.author: Heinz Guderian
sources.notes: Guderian's account of the development of German armoured doctrine and his wartime command.

sources.title: Lost Victories (Verlorene Siege)
sources.author: Erich von Manstein
sources.notes: Manstein's account of his operations on the Eastern Front; invaluable for Stalingrad and Kharkov.

sources.title: The German Army at War, 1939–1945
sources.author: Martin van Creveld
sources.notes: Analysis of Wehrmacht operational effectiveness and logistics.

sources.title: Hitler's Generals
sources.author: Correlli Barnett (ed.)
sources.notes: Biographical essays on 20 senior Wehrmacht and Luftwaffe commanders.

sources.title: Bundesarchiv-Militärarchiv, Freiburg im Breisgau
sources.notes: German Federal Military Archive; primary source for unit records, personnel files, and operational orders.

sources.title: U-Boat Archive (U-Boot-Archiv), Cuxhaven
sources.notes: Specialist archive for Kriegsmarine submarine records, war diaries, and crew testimonies.

sources.title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich
sources.author: William L. Shirer
sources.notes: Comprehensive political history of the Nazi regime by an American journalist who was present in Berlin.

sources.title: Stalingrad
sources.author: Antony Beevor
sources.notes: Definitive account of the Battle of Stalingrad drawing on Soviet archives.

sources.title: D-Day: The Battle for Normandy
sources.author: Antony Beevor
sources.notes: Comprehensive account of the Normandy campaign from both Allied and German perspectives.
